assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' for: single license filter (criterion-1), comma-separated filter (criterion-2), 400 validation (criterion-3), pagination integration (criterion-4), and unchanged response shape (criterion-5). The report summary confirms all five: 'Single license filtering', 'Multi-license filtering', 'Invalid license rejection', 'Pagination integration', 'Response shape'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files (`list.rs`, `mod.rs`) and 1 new file (`package.rs`) align with Files to Modify and Files to Create'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR'. report.md line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No evidence anywhere in the outputs of sub-task creation.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are report/analysis files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md). The report only describes reading and analyzing the diff. No evidence of file modifications, code edits, or write operations to source files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with 'Overall: PASS' and a summary of findings. There is no merge action, no 'gh pr merge' command, and no indication of auto-merging. The report format presents findings for human review with the closing note 'This comment was AI-generated by [sdlc-workflow/verify-pr]'.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (`tests/api/package.rs` with 4 test functions); no modified or deleted tests'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md contains all expected verdicts: Intent Alignment domain: Scope Containment (line 7, PASS), Diff Size (line 8, PASS), Commit Traceability (line 9, PASS). Security domain: Sensitive Patterns (line 10, PASS). Correctness domain: CI Status (line 11, PASS), Acceptance Criteria (line 12, PASS), Verification Commands (line 15, N/A). Style/Conventions domain: Test Quality (line 13, PASS — combining 'no repetitive tests detected', 'doc comments', and 'Eval Quality: N/A'), Test Change Classification (line 14, ADDITIVE).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment: report.md line 7 specifies '2 modified files (`list.rs`, `mod.rs`) and 1 new file (`package.rs`) align with Files to Modify and Files to Create'. Security: report.md line 10 states 'No secrets, credentials, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'. Correctness: criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive per-criterion code-level verification with code snippets, query parameter parsing details, test assertions, and filter-then-paginate ordering analysis. The report summary (lines 22-25) also provides code-level evidence for each acceptance criterion. Style/Conventions: report.md line 13 details 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each tests distinct behavior); Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 14 details 'Only new test file added (`tests/api/package.rs` with 4 test functions); no modified or deleted tests'.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each tests distinct behavior); Eval Quality: N/A'. Eval Quality is explicitly marked N/A within the Test Quality verdict and does not affect the overall Test Quality result (which is PASS based on the other sub-checks).

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 13). No sub-tasks are created anywhere in the outputs. report.md line 6 confirms 'No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No evidence of eval failure sub-task creation in any output file.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These appear as separate rows in the summary table (lines 5-6), distinct from the domain-specific verdicts (lines 7-15), confirming they are determined independently from domain analysis.
